summary: Powerful second set with China>Rider and soaring Stella Blue
blurb: The concert moves from a tight first set into an explosive second set highlighted by the China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, a Terrapin Station jam that erupts into Jackhammer, and a lyrical Stella Blue. Jerry’s lead lines sparkle on Loser while Phil unleashes a bass tsunami on The Other One. Excellent audience tapes capture the high‑energy performance with crystal‑clear instrument separation.
review: On March 2, 1992 at Atlanta’s Omni the Dead delivered one of those rare nights where everything clicks. A concise first set opens with a fiery “Cold Rain And Snow” and rolls through tight renditions of “Little Red Rooster,” “Brown Eyed Women,” and a heartfelt “Loser” that lets Garcia stretch his lead voice. The real fireworks ignite in the second set: a seamless “China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider” launch into a playful “Man Smart, Woman Smarter,” then a monster Terrapin Station jam that morphs into a thunderous Jackhammer climax before flowing into a transcendent “Space → The Other One.” The band rides the groove straight into an soaring “Stella Blue” and a rollicking “Around & Around,” closing with a heartfelt “Knockin’ On Heaven’s Door.” Reviewers consistently note Jerry’s exuberant lead work, Phil’s bass tsunami on The Other One, and the twin‑keyboard force of Vince Welnick and Bruce Hornsby that adds a lush, unpredictable texture. Mickey and Bill keep the rhythmic engine roaring throughout, especially during the Jackhammer section. Two audience recordings are praised as “excellent,” capturing the ambience and energy with minimal noise, making this show a must‑listen for fans who love high‑energy late‑’80s/early‑’90s Dead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are marked positive and one mixed, with multiple reviewers praising standout sequences (China>Rider, Terrapin>Jackhammer, Stella Blue) and noting high energy, clear instrument separation, and strong band interplay. No consistent negative comments appear, warranting a solid positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience tape with crystal‑clear instrument separation, minimal noise, and the most detailed notes on standout second‑set moments.

key_highlights: Powerful China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Terrapin Station jam that builds into a Jackhammer climax
Soaring Stella Blue that caps an inspired second set
Vince/Welnick keyboard duo delivering a force‑of‑nature sound

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia let loose on Loser and added soaring lead lines throughout, driving the night’s energy.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass bomb opening The Other One was described as a tsunami of low‑end power.
band_performance.Brent: The twin‑keyboard attack of Vince Welnick and Bruce Hornsby acted as a force of nature, trading playful lines and adding lush texture.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming stayed high‑energy, especially during the Jackhammer section.
band_performance.Billy: Bill matched Mickey’s intensity, providing tight rhythmic support that propelled the second set.
